dependencies: Reliance on third parties for supply of pharmaceutical and other products
Concentration of suppliers - few large suppliers control significant share of pharmaceuticals market
Largest supplier accounted for 11% of total purchases in fiscal 2025
Ten largest suppliers accounted for approximately 69% of total purchases
risks: Supplier failure to satisfy production demand

Supply disruptions from product rationalization, government actions, trade sanctions, tariffs, labor disputes, supplier financial distress, natural disasters, civil unrest, military conflicts, epidemics or pandemics
Changes in supplier pricing, selling, inventory, distribution or supply policies
Difficulties in sourcing products due to compliance challenges, access to raw materials, manufacturing shutdowns
Concentration risk - loss of large supplier could adversely affect business
Generic drug manufacturers may not fully indemnify against patent infringement claims
Tariffs and trade restrictions may require substantial additional sourcing costs or alternate supply sources

regulatory_risks: DEA, FDA, HHS, CMS oversight and compliance requirements for pharmaceutical distribution
Controlled Substances Act compliance and monitoring requirements
Healthcare fraud and abuse laws (Anti-Kickback Statute, Stark Law, False Claims Act)
Drug Supply Chain Security Act (DSCSA) interoperable unit-level traceability requirements
State and federal licensing requirements for wholesale drug distributors
Privacy and data protection laws (HIPAA, GDPR, PIPEDA, state privacy laws)
Cybersecurity incident reporting requirements (Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act, SEC reporting)
Environmental laws and regulations (discharge of pollutants, hazardous waste management, contaminated site cleanup)
Climate change regulations and disclosure requirements
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act compliance
Healthcare reform impacts (ACA, Inflation Reduction Act drug pricing provisions)
340B Drug Pricing Program restrictions and litigation
State opioid taxes and assessments (New York Opioid Stewardship Act)
Provincial government drug pricing regulations in Canada
FDA product safety and efficacy regulations
Government contract procurement laws and compliance requirements
AI and generative AI regulatory guidance
Value Added Tax (VAT) assessments in foreign jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Trade sanctions and tariffs may disrupt supply chain and increase costs
Government requisition, diversion or allocation of inventory during geopolitical events
Retaliatory tariffs or trade restrictions imposed by other countries
Military conflicts and civil unrest may disrupt operations
